creator: ecki@zusammenkunft.net
text:
I head some errors with "metadata updates" when running portsnap (from 9.1-PRE) fetch, so I cleaned all of /var/db/portsnap/* and retried:

# portsnap fetch
Looking up portsnap.FreeBSD.org mirrors... 7 mirrors found.
Fetching public key from ec2-eu-west-1.portsnap.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching snapshot tag from ec2-eu-west-1.portsnap.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching snapshot metadata... done.
Fetching snapshot generated at Wed Jan 28 01:15:18 CET 2015:
f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0  0% of   70 MB    0  Bps^[[B^[[B
fetch: transfer timed out
fetch: f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz appears to be truncated: 0/73884622 bytes
root@neskaya /var/db/portsnap# ls -l
total 24
-rw-r--r--  1 root  wheel    0 Jan 28 03:43 f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
# cat serverlist_tried
1 10 ec2-eu-west-1.portsnap.freebsd.org
# cat serverlist
1 10 ec2-eu-west-1.portsnap.freebsd.org
2 10 sourcefire.portsnap.freebsd.org
2 10 your-org.portsnap.freebsd.org
3 10 isc.portsnap.freebsd.org
4 10 ec2-ap-northeast-1.portsnap.freebsd.org
4 10 ec2-ap-southeast-2.portsnap.freebsd.org
4 10 ec2-sa-east-1.portsnap.freebsd.org

The mirror in question seems to be broken, because when using "portsnap -s isc.freebsd.org fetch" it worked:

# portsnap -s isc.portsnap.freebsd.org fetch
Looking up isc.portsnap.freebsd.org mirrors... none found.
Fetching snapshot tag from isc.portsnap.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching snapshot metadata... done.
Fetching snapshot generated at Wed Jan 28 01:15:18 CET 2015:
f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0  0% of   70 MB    0  Bps
fetch: http://isc.portsnap.freebsd.org/s/f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz: Bad file descriptor
fetch: f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz: Bad file descriptor
# rm f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0556c7f3e63fb89bf1c.tgz
# portsnap -s isc.portsnap.freebsd.org fetch       Looking up isc.portsnap.freebsd.org mirrors... none found.
Fetching snapshot tag from isc.portsnap.freebsd.org... done.
Fetching snapshot metadata... done.
Fetching snapshot generated at Wed Jan 28 01:15:18 CET 2015:
f668cb3e3d9b4acf1350f3f471b8827848765fa86759e0100% of   70 MB 1114 kBps 00m00s
...



BTW: i really wonder why portsnap cannot recognize such broken mirrors and select another automatically (the cronjobs have failed a few days unnoticed, me bad). In this case there was even a timeout. (tried multiple times all failed). I also wonder why it does not load .tmp files and rename them after verify, especially if it cannot recover from them ("bad file descriptor" above).
